Let me write a more robust extractor:
```js
function mediaHoverNoneBody(): string {
  // Find "@media ... (hover: none) ... {" — allow extra query conditions.
  const open = CSS.search(/@media[^{]*\(\s*hover\s*:\s*none\s*\)[^{]*\{/);
  if (open < 0) return '';
  // Find the first '{' at/after `open`.
  const braceIdx = CSS.indexOf('{', open);
  if (braceIdx < 0) return '';
  let i = braceIdx + 1;
  let depth = 1;
  let body = '';
  while (i < CSS.length && depth > 0) {
    const ch = CSS[i];
    if (ch === '{') depth++;
    else if (ch === '}') depth--;
    if (depth > 0) body += ch;
    i++;
  }
  return body;
}
```
The `CSS.search(/@media[^{]*\(\s*hover\s*:\s*none\s*\)[^{]*\{/)` — `[^{]*` ensures we don't cross a brace, and matches `@media (hover: none) {` or `@media (hover: none) and (pointer: coarse) {`. Good. This handles the common cases including the reviewer's exact example.
